title: Measurement of XylE Enzyme Activity by Microplate Reader
author: Ji Gao
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7suhnew

steps.title: Shake the plate for 00:00:05 at 600 rpm
steps.instruction: Shake the plate for 00:00:05 at 600 rpm .

steps.title: Measure the absorbance of cell culture...
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance of cell culture at 600 nm as the estimation of cell amounts.

steps.title: Plate out and add 2
steps.instruction: Plate out and add 2.5 µL catechol ( 10 millimolar (mM) ) to every well, then plate in immediately. The working concentration of substrate is 0.25 millimolar (mM) here. Other working concentration could also work.

steps.title: Shake the plate for 00:00:05 at 600 rpm
steps.instruction: Shake the plate for 00:00:05 at 600 rpm .

steps.title: Measure the absorbance of cell culture...
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance of cell culture at 377 nm, continuously (like 00:00:10 per reading) or after a certain period of time (like 00:01:30 ).

steps.title: Add 100 µL cell culture per well
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL cell culture per well. It is recommanded to use replicates and controls to avoid mistakes or deviation.

steps.title: Measure the absorbance of cell culture...
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance of cell culture at 377 nm as the baseline of A377 before the reaction.

steps.title: Normalize the A337 value by optical density at 600 nm
steps.instruction: Normalize the A337 value by optical density at 600 nm. Make a Time-A377 plot like this: Or simply substract the absorbance before the reaction from the value after a specifc period of time:

steps.title: Turn on the microplate reader and computer
steps.instruction: Turn on the microplate reader and computer. Run the software to set the protocol and plate layout.

steps.title: Export the data from the software
steps.instruction: Export the data from the software. Properly dispose of the contaminated cell culture and microplates!
